mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off positioning as investors price in higher recession probabilities, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion (RSAFS) combined with contracting industrial production (INDPRO) signals a demand-driven supply bottleneck that boosts corporate pricing power and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when RSAFS rises and INDPRO falls.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator for durable goods consumption and construction-related corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days specifically when real GDP growth exceeds 2.5 percent annually.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with robust housing activity
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of observations where HOUST rises alongside GDPC1 growth above 2.5%, invalidating the leading indicator premise.

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in the industrial production index reflects immediate capacity utilization improvements and stronger corporate throughput, which directly translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: The signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year backtest, or 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant premium during INDPRO expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5 percent, borrowing costs suppress corporate investment and consumer leverage, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays above 3.5%, or the signal exhibits no predictive power after controlling for equity volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth (PAYEMS) coupled with stable consumer credit balances (RSAFS) indicates healthy wage-driven consumption without debt-fueled fragility, creating a high-probability environment for positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive R-squared by at least 2% over a single-proxy baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer credit expansion (RSAFS) signals imminent retail sales strength, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days specifically during periods where core inflation (proxied by CPIAUCSL) remains below 3.0 percent year-over-year.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when RSAFS accelerates alongside CPIAUCSL growth under 3.0%, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 3-year walk-forward test.
